value_proposition: Turn should-we-build decisions into a fast, testable, human-authorized workflow before coding agents consume budget.
target_segment: Product and innovation teams using coding agents

target_user.description: A product lead responsible for discovery and delivery decisions.
target_user.value_or_need: A concise way to identify the next experiment and prevent unauthorized implementation.

economic_buyer.description: A product, innovation, or engineering leader who owns the MVP budget.
economic_buyer.value_or_need: Reduced waste and decision evidence that survives handoffs and audits.

willingness_to_pay_or_internal_roi_hypot: A team will adopt the workflow if it prevents at least one unnecessary multi-day implementation cycle per quarter.
revenue_or_economic_value_model: Value is measured as avoided engineering time plus faster evidence-backed investment decisions.
acquisition_distribution_hypothesis: Teams will adopt TEMPO through a repository template and a one-command judge/demo workflow.
adoption_friction: Teams must express business assumptions as structured artifacts
A human must explicitly approve the decision brief and implementation warrant

alternatives_and_differentiation.differentiation: TEMPO binds evidence, scope, budget, deadline, and human authority to machine-enforced hashes.

defensibility_hypothesis: The durable advantage is a portable decision ledger and deterministic enforcement contract rather than a proprietary prompt.

material_risks.description: A synthetic demo could be mistaken for real customer validation.
